title: Breadth-First Search, Queues, and Unweighted Shortest Paths · Runtime Lens: Unweighted Optimality
summary: The queue uses slow front deletion, predecessors change after discovery, distances mix cells and pixels, and shortest is claimed on weighted edges. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the unweighted optimality increment in deque-based shortest-path solver. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain why BFS minimizes edge count only when every admitted passage has equal cost.
Assign distance and predecessor at first discovery and reconstruct a valid route after reaching the goal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-bfs-shortest-path-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-bfs-shortest-path-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_bfs_shortest_path_2(reported_edges: int = 18, oracle_edges: int = 18, discovered: int = 39, predecessor_links: int = 38, fifo_order_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if reported_edges < 0 or oracle_edges < 0 or discovered < 1:
        return False, reported_edges, "bfs-count"
    if reported_edges != oracle_edges:
        return False, reported_edges, "nonshortest-route"
    if predecessor_links != discovered - 1:
        return False, predecessor_links, "predecessor-map"
    if not fifo_order_valid:
        return False, discovered, "frontier-order"
    assert reported_edges == oracle_edges
    return True, reported_edges, "shortest-unweighted-route"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Explain why BFS minimizes edge count only when every admitted passage has equal cost.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Unweighted Optimality model · Theory Unweighted Optima…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Unweighted Optimality precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Unweighted Optimality model · Theory Unweighted Optima…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 1669 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove an unweighted shortest route through FIFO layers and stable predecessors; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain why BFS minimizes edge count only when every admitted passage has equal cost.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Unweighted Optimality model · Theory Unweighted Optima…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: BFS always finds the cheapest route through any maze.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-unweighted-optimality-wm06q2.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-unweighted-optimality-wm06q2(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_unweighted_optimality_wm06q2\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reported_edges < 0 or oracle_edges < 0 or discovered < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reported_edges != oracle_edges)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?predecessor_links != discovered - 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not fifo_order_valid)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The decision applies Unweighted Optimality to the changed stakeholder case.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using minimizes and edge plus specific case evidence.
